summary: First end-to-end pass of Sinhala Novel Reading App. Backend pytest suite (42 cases) covers auth, novels, chapters, ratings, subscriptions, ads, reminders, TTS (real OpenAI via Emergent), security, admin RBAC and rate-limits — 40/42 passing. The 2 failures expose ONE real bug: services/access.py:is_user_premium() only checks the Subscription table and ignores user.premium_status, so the seeded admin (premium_status=True but no Subscription row) cannot read premium chapters nor fetch /api/security/content-key. Admin web frontend (Login, Overview, Novels, Users, Push, Mobile Preview) loads and works; sidebar contains 6 items and NO Settings link as required.

critical.issue: Admin (seeded with users.premium_status=True) is treated as NON-premium because services/access.py:is_user_premium() only looks at the Subscription table and never reads the user.premium_status flag. Result: admin sees locked premium chapters (locked:true, empty content) and gets 403 from /api/security/content-key. Subscribed users work correctly.

minor.issue: k8s ingress strips the CF-IPCountry header so geo detection only works via X-Country-Code in this environment. Production CF will likely set it. Consider documenting / adding X-Forwarded-Country fallback.

issues: Test did not detect an explicit 'Admin' role badge on the admin row; role column shows lowercase 'user'/'admin'. Not blocking but spec expected a styled Admin badge.

action_items: Fix services/access.py:is_user_premium() — also return True when user.premium_status is True (or, alternatively, have the seed create a perpetual Subscription row for the admin). This unblocks admin reading premium chapters and /api/security/content-key.
Optional: add a styled 'Admin' role badge in Users.js for users with role=='admin'.
Optional: document or auto-translate alternate country headers (X-Forwarded-Country) for paywall-config geo detection behind k8s ingress.
critical_code_review_comments: is_user_premium() should respect the explicit user.premium_status flag set by seed/admin promotion. Currently the flag is dead weight on the User model.
Rate-limit storage is in-memory per worker; fine for single-worker dev but document that it resets on redeploy.
TTS endpoint accepts arbitrary model names and returns 502 on invalid value; consider validating against the supported list ('tts-1', 'tts-1-hd') in the request schema for a cleaner 400.
test_credentials: Admin: ruvinda@sinhalanovels.app / Ruvinda@2026 (seeded). Test users are auto-created per test with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Tests register fresh users (TEST_user_*, TEST_sub_*, TEST_ideamart_*, TEST_wh_*, TEST_ads_*, TEST_premread_*, TEST_ck_*) and one TEST_Novel that is cleaned up in-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. The 2 failing tests (TestChapters::test_premium_chapter_open_for_admin, TestSecurity::test_content_key_premium) are intentional regression tests for the premium-status bug — once main agent fixes is_user_premium to honour user.premium_status, these should turn green. If you run the login_rate_limit test, the auth login endpoint will be 429 for ~60s — wait a minute before any subsequent admin login UI tests.
rca of the issue: BUG RCA: app/services/access.py:is_user_premium(db, user_id) returns True only when a Subscription row with status='active' and (expires_at is None or in the future) exists. The seed (app/services/seed.py) sets User.premium_status=True on the admin but never inserts a Subscription row. Every paywall check (chapters router line ~30-50 and security/content-key) calls is_user_premium() → False for admin → locked chapter / 403. FIX: update is_user_premium to also return True when the user record has premium_status=True (or role='admin'), OR have the seed create a non-expiring Subscription(provider='comp', plan='lifetime', status='active', expires_at=None) for any admin/comp-premium user. REPRODUCTION: 1) login admin via /api/auth/login (premium_status=True returned). 2) GET /api/chapters/<premium-id> with admin token -> {locked:true, content:''}. 3) GET /api/security/content-key with admin token -> 403.
